name: All purpose cleaner
ratio: 50 ml white vinegar, 30 g baking soda, 50 ml soap
steps: Pour the white vinegar into the pot, add the baking soda, stir until dissolved, then add the soap, stir evenly, and it is ready to use.

name: glass cleaner
ratio: 200 ml alcohol, 100 ml white vinegar, 100 ml water
steps: Pour alcohol, white vinegar, and water into a container, stir evenly, and put it into a sprayer to use.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: kitchen grease cleaner
ratio: 30g baking soda, 20ml dish soap, 500ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda in warm water, add dish soap and mix well. Spray on the oily surface, let it sit for 5 minutes and then wipe with a rag.

name: Bathroom descaler
ratio: 20g citric acid, 15g baking soda, 300ml water
steps: Dissolve citric acid and baking soda in water, stir until completely dissolved, and pour into a spray bottle. Spray on the scale and let it sit for 10 minutes, then scrub, and finally rinse with water.
safetyNote: It is recommended to wear gloves when operating and avoid prolonged contact with skin.

name: furniture maintenance wax
ratio: 30 g beeswax, 100 ml olive oil, 20 ml turpentine
steps: Heat beeswax over water until completely melted, add olive oil and turpentine, stir evenly. After it cools down slightly, apply a small amount and wipe the surface of the furniture, then polish with a dry cloth.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable and has a pungent smell. Please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: carpet stain remover
ratio: 100 ml white vinegar, 10 ml dish soap, 400 ml warm water
steps: Mix white vinegar, dish soap and warm water and stir until foamy. Use a soft-bristled brush dipped in foam to gently brush the stained area, then use a clean damp cloth to absorb excess water.

name: silverware polish
ratio: 30g baking soda, 15g salt, 1 piece of aluminum foil, 500ml hot water
steps: Place crumpled aluminum foil on the bottom of the container, add baking soda and salt, pour in hot water and stir to dissolve. Soak the silverware for 5 to 10 minutes, then rinse and dry it to restore its luster.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when operating in hot water. Silverware should not be soaked for too long to avoid damaging the carvings.

name: Air conditioning filter cleaner
ratio: 15 ml dish soap, 50 ml white vinegar, 1000 ml warm water
steps: Add dish soap and white vinegar to warm water and mix well. Remove the filter and soak it for 15 minutes. Brush lightly with a soft brush, rinse with water, dry in the shade and reinstall it.
safetyNote: Be sure to disconnect the power supply of the air conditioner before cleaning, and do not put it back in before the filter is completely dry.

name: Tile gap cleaner
ratio: 100 ml hydrogen peroxide, 30 g baking soda
steps: Mix baking soda and hydrogen peroxide into a paste and dip the old toothbrush into the gap in the brushing tile. Let stand for 10 minutes, then wipe off with a damp cloth, and finally wipe the surface with water.
safetyNote: Hydrogen peroxide is bleachable, so try applying it inconspicuously first and avoid touching colored tile surfaces.

name: Stainless steel cleaner
ratio: 30 g flour, 50 ml white vinegar, 200 ml warm water
steps: Mix the flour with warm water and stir well with white vinegar. Wipe the stainless steel surface with a soft cloth, polish it in the direction of the texture, and then wipe it clean with water.

name: Microwave oven deodorant
ratio: 1 lemon, 200 ml water
steps: Slice the lemon into a heat-resistant bowl and add the water. Heat in a microwave oven over high heat for 3 minutes, let stand for 5 minutes and then remove. Wipe the lumen with a damp cloth to remove odor.
safetyNote: The bowl is very hot after heating, please use insulated gloves when taking it out.

name: Kettle scale remover
ratio: 30 g citric acid, 500 ml water
steps: Dissolve citric acid in water Pour into a kettle, heat to a microwave and soak for 30 minutes after powering off. Pour the solution and rinse twice to remove scale.
safetyNote: Do not close the lid of the kettle when removing scale to avoid excessive steam pressure. Not for use with aluminum kettles.

name: Bathroom Mold Remover
ratio: 50 ml bleach, 450 ml water
steps: Bleach is diluted with water and sprayed into a spray bottle on a moldy wall or crevice. Let stand for 15 minutes, then brush, rinse with water and keep ventilated and dry.
safetyNote: Bleach is corrosive and irritating, please wear gloves, keep ventilated, and do not mix with acid detergents.

name: oven cleaner.
ratio: 50g baking soda, 15ml dish soap, 100ml warm water
steps: Add a small amount of warm water to the baking soda to make a thick paste, and apply it to the oil stain on the inner wall of the oven. After standing overnight, wipe off with a damp cloth and wash with detergent, then dry.
safetyNote: Be sure to power off and cool before cleaning to avoid contact of the cleaner with the heating tube.

name: Faucet glow agent
ratio: Toothpaste 15 g, baking soda 10 g
steps: Mix the toothpaste with baking soda to form a paste, and wipe the surface of the faucet with a soft cloth. Polish in the direction of the arc, rinse with water and polish with a dry cloth.

name: Leather sofa cleaner
ratio: 30 ml olive oil, 20 ml white vinegar, 100 ml water
steps: Mix the three ingredients evenly and gently wipe the leather surface with a soft cloth. After wiping, take a clean wet cloth to wipe off the residue, and the leather will regain its luster after drying naturally.
safetyNote: Try rubbing in an inconspicuous place before use to avoid discoloration.

name: Shoe deodorant spray
ratio: 80 ml alcohol, 3 ml tea tree oil, 20 ml water
steps: Mix the alcohol with the essential oil, shake with water and fill into a spray bottle. Spray 2-3 times inside each time after wearing, and deodorize and sterilize after drying in the shade.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable and away from sources of ignition. Try spraying new shoes or valuable materials first.

name: Screen cleaner
ratio: Dish soap 10 ml, white vinegar 30 ml, warm water 500 ml
steps: Dissolve dish soap and white vinegar in warm water and brush both sides of the window with a soft bristle brush. After brushing, rinse with clean water, dry and reassemble.

name: Brass Vessel Polish
ratio: 30 ml lemon juice, 10 g salt, 20 g flour
steps: Mix salt with flour and add lemon juice to paste. Apply to the brass surface and let it stand for 10 minutes. Wipe off the soft cloth and rinse with water to dry.

name: Machine lubricant
ratio: 200 ml gasoline, 100 ml soap
steps: Pour the gasoline into the container, add the soap solution, and stir to use.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated place away from open flames and children.

name: Bicycle Chain Lubricant
ratio: 50 g liquid paraffin, 50 ml gasoline
steps: Mix liquid paraffin with gasoline and stir to use.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated place away from open flames and children.

name: Door and window hinge lubricant
ratio: Vaseline 20g, oil 30ml
steps: Put the petrolatum and the oil in a heat-resistant container and heat it in a waterproof way until it is fully fused. After cooling, dip it with a cotton swab and apply it to the hinge shaft.
safetyNote: Waste oil may contain impurities and is recommended for lubrication of non-precision parts only.

name: Leather care oil
ratio: Vaseline 50g, Lanolin 20g, Parfum 2ml
steps: Heat and melt petrolatum and lanolin in water. Add essence and stir well after extinguishing. After cooling, apply a small amount to the leather surface and wipe evenly with a soft cloth.

name: Sewing machine lubricating oil
ratio: 80 ml white oil, 20 ml alcohol
steps: Pour the white oil into a clean container and stir in the alcohol. Use a dropper to take a small amount of drops into the moving parts of the sewing machine, and turn it several times to distribute the oil evenly.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: Clock lubricant
ratio: 90 ml white oil, 5 g cetacean
steps: Chop the cetacean into white oil and stir in water until completely dissolved. After cooling, dip a small amount with a very fine needle tube and apply it to the bearing of the watch. Do not overdo it.
safetyNote: Precision watches are recommended to be maintained by a professional and self-lubricating may result in travel anomalies.

name: Wire rope grease
ratio: Grease 50g, Kerosene 30ml
steps: Place the grease into the container and gradually add kerosene, stirring and diluting to a brushable consistency. Apply evenly to the surface of the wire rope, running several times to allow the grease to penetrate between the strands.
safetyNote: Kerosene is flammable, please operate in a ventilated place away from sources of ignition. Waste oil should be properly recycled.

name: Wooden Drawer Rail Oil
ratio: Beeswax 15 g, linseed oil 50 ml
steps: Heat the beeswax in water and melt, add linseed oil and stir well. After cooling, apply a small amount to the wooden drawer slides and track grooves and push and pull several times.

name: Fan Bearing Lubricant
ratio: Lubricating oil 40 ml, kerosene 10 ml
steps: Mix the lubricating oil and kerosene evenly, and use a dropper to take 1-2 drops and inject them into the fan bearing. Manually rotate the fan blades several times to distribute the oil, wipe off the excess oil stains, and then power on the test run.
safetyNote: Be sure to disconnect the power supply before operation, kerosene is flammable and away from the fire source.

name: Iron antirust oil
ratio: 80 ml motor oil, 20 ml wax oil
steps: Mix the oil with the wax oil and stir well. Apply a thin brush to a clean and dry iron surface, and leave to dry to form a protective film to slow down corrosion.
safetyNote: Waste oil may contain harmful substances, please wear gloves when operating to avoid contact with the skin.

name: Mold release oil
ratio: 100ml cooking oil, 10g beeswax
steps: The beeswax is heated and melted in water, and the cooking oil is added and stirred evenly. After cooling, it can be applied to the inner wall of the mold with a brush to facilitate the demolding of concrete or gypsum products.

name: Zipper lubricating wax
ratio: Candles 10g, Vaseline 15g
steps: The candles are chopped and heated to melt in water with vaseline and stirred evenly. After cooling, dip it with a cotton swab, apply it between the zipper teeth, and pull it several times to make it smooth.

name: Instrument String Shaft Lubricant
ratio: Graphite powder 2 g, vegetable oil 20 ml
steps: Add the graphite powder to the vegetable oil and stir well. Apply a small amount to the threads of the string shaft with a toothpick, and screw in and out several times to distribute the oil film evenly.
safetyNote: The amount should be very small to prevent oil from penetrating the sound board and affecting the tone.

name: Skateboard wheel bearing oil
ratio: Lubricating oil 15 ml, detergent 5 ml
steps: After removing the bearing and removing the dust, drip 1-2 drops of lubricating oil. Manually rotate the bearing to allow the oil to penetrate, wipe off any excess grease on the surface and put it back in.
safetyNote: The detergent is flammable and operated away from sources of ignition.

name: Antirust oil for garden tools
ratio: 100 ml used oil, 30 ml diesel
steps: Waste oil is evenly mixed with diesel, and coated with a brush on the metal edges and joints of tools such as shovels and shears. It can be stored after drying to slow down corrosion.
safetyNote: Waste oil contains harmful substances, please wear gloves and store it properly to avoid contact with children.

name: Printer Rail Lubricant
ratio: 20 ml silicone oil
steps: After the power is off, clean the guide rail dust, and apply a small amount of silicone oil to the surface of the slide rail with a cotton swab. Manually push the print head back and forth several times, wipe off the excess silicone oil and power on the test machine.
safetyNote: Do not use too much to prevent silicone oil from contaminating the printed components.

name: Fishing reel grease
ratio: Grease 20g, Vaseline 10g
steps: Mix the grease with the petrolatum, and fill a small amount into the gear mesh of the fishing line gear. Spin the wheel for a few turns to distribute the grease and wipe away the excess.

name: Lock core lubricant
ratio: Graphite powder 3 g, oil 10 ml
steps: The graphite powder is mixed with the engine oil into a thin paste and dripped into the lock core hole with a straw. Turn the lock cylinder by repeatedly inserting and removing the key so that the lubricant covers the sprung mechanism evenly.
safetyNote: Excess liquid oil should not be used to avoid clogging of the lock core due to dust adsorption.

name: Watercolor Pigments
ratio: Gelatin 50g, Pigment 10g, Water 400ml
steps: Add the gelatin powder to water, heat until the gelatin is dissolved, add the pigment, and stir to use.
safetyNote: Please be careful of scalding when heating, it is recommended to operate under adult supervision.

name: oil paints
ratio: 100ml linseed oil, 20g pigment, 5g rosin, 5ml dry concoction
steps: Pour the linseed oil into the container, add the pigment and rosin, stir well, then add the dry concoction, stir well and use.

name: Pastel Pigments
ratio: Gypsum powder 30g, gum arabic 5g, moderate amount of pigment
steps: Dissolve the arabic gum in a small amount of warm water, add gypsum powder and pigments, grind repeatedly with a grinding board to a fine paste, and put in a palette to dry.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling dust when grinding and wear a mask.

name: Lime mural paint
ratio: 200g lime slurry, 10g pigment, 5g hemp knife
steps: Add the pigment to the lime slurry and stir until the color is even, then add the hemp knife fiber and stir. That is, you can use a brush to paint on the wall or on the mural.
safetyNote: Lime is alkaline and should be operated with gloves and goggles.

name: Acrylic Paints
ratio: Propylene Lotion 100ml, Pigment 15g, Water 30ml
steps: Gradually add the pigment to the acrylic emulsion and stir evenly with a palette knife. Depending on the consistency, adjust the amount of water and adjust it to a state suitable for brushing.

name: Tempera paints
ratio: 1 egg yolk, 5g pigment, 10ml water
steps: Remove the protein from the egg yolk and add a small amount of water and stir to form an emulsion. Gradually add the pigment to grind until it is fine and even, that is, it can be used for drawing wooden boards or mural eggs.
safetyNote: Pigments need to be selected from mineral pigments, which are used now and should not be kept for a long time.

name: Homemade ink
ratio: Carbon Black 10g, Gum Arabic 5g, Glycerin 5ml, Water 100ml
steps: Arabic gum is dissolved in warm water, carbon black is added and repeatedly grinded until there are no fine particles, and then glycerol is added and stirred evenly. Fill the ink bottle after filtration and let it stand for 1 day before use.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling dust when grinding carbon black and wear a mask.

name: Pastels
ratio: Gypsum powder 100g, pigment 5g, water 40ml
steps: The gypsum powder is evenly mixed with the pigment, gradually added with water and kneaded into a mass, and filled into a chalk mold to compact. Remove and dry in the shade for 2-3 days, and then use after fully curing.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling dust when the gypsum powder is dry, and tap to avoid fragmentation when the mold is demoulded.

name: Latex paint for wall
ratio: 200 g lime, 10 g pigment, 50 ml white latex, 300 ml water
steps: The lime is mixed with water to form a paste, the pigment is added and stirred evenly, and then the white latex is mixed to adjust the adhesion. After adjusting to a brushable consistency, paint the wall with a roller or brush.
safetyNote: Lime is alkaline and should be handled with gloves and a mask.

name: Metal antirust paint
ratio: Alkyd Resin 100ml, Pigment 15g, Turpentine 30ml
steps: Add the pigment to the alkyd resin, grind and stir well, then add turpentine to adjust the consistency. Brush on the metal surface after rust removal, and brush twice after 24 hours of natural drying.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable and irritating, please operate in a ventilated place away from sources of ignition.

name: Fabric dye solution
ratio: Dye 10g, Salt 30g, White Vinegar 20ml, Water 1000ml
steps: Add salt and dye to water, heat to 60°C, stir to dissolve, add white vinegar. After wetting the fabric, put it in the dyeing solution, stir and dye for 30 minutes, rinse with water until the water is clean and dry.
safetyNote: Pay attention to anti-scalding when heating the dye, the dye may dye the skin, it is recommended to wear gloves.

name: Mural primer
ratio: Lide Powder 100g, Glue 30ml, Water 200ml
steps: Dissolve the glue in water and gradually add lide powder and stir until the paste is even. Use a scraper to thinly coat the wall as a mural primer, and then color it after drying it.
safetyNote: Avoid breathing dust when stirring and wear a mask.

name: Asphalt paint
ratio: 50 g bitumen, 30 ml turpentine, 10 g pigment
steps: The asphalt is heated to a flowing state, and turpentine and pigments are added and stirred evenly. Adjust to a consistency suitable for scraping, that is, it can be used for wooden board or canvas creation.
safetyNote: Asphalt and turpentine are both irritating and flammable, please operate in a ventilated place away from sources of ignition.

name: Spray paint dilute paint
ratio: 100 ml acrylic paint, 20 ml diluent, 10 ml water
steps: The propylene paint is added with a diluent and stirred evenly, and the spray consistency is adjusted according to the appropriate amount of water. Filtered and loaded into a spray gun or canister, thinly sprayed multiple times to obtain a uniform coating.
safetyNote: The diluent is flammable and toxic and must be handled in a well-ventilated place and wearing a protective mask.

name: Blackboard paint
ratio: 200g cement paint, 5g pigment, 50ml water
steps: Adjust the cement paint with water to a brushable consistency and add the black pigment and stir evenly. Brush twice on a flat surface and write in chalk after drying.

name: Wood colorants
ratio: 50 g walnut peel, 100 ml vinegar, 300 ml water
steps: Decoct walnut zest in water for 30 minutes, strain and add vinegar. Brush on polished wood surfaces while hot or cool for a natural tan.
safetyNote: Burn carefully when decocting, the coloring depth can be adjusted by the number of brush passes.

name: Enamel paint
ratio: 20g borax, 30g quartz powder, 8g pigment
steps: Dry mix the borax with quartz powder and add the pigment to grind until fine. Add a small amount of water to form a glaze, which is applied to the metal matrix and then fired at high temperature to develop color.
safetyNote: The firing should be carried out in a professional kiln, and protective equipment should be worn during operation.

name: Stage Makeup Pigments
ratio: 30 g cornstarch, moderate amount of food coloring, 10 ml glycerin
steps: The corn starch is mixed with glycerin to form a paste, and the food color is gradually added to the desired color. It can be applied directly to the skin and removed with warm water.
safetyNote: For external use only, please take a skin test for sensitive skin.

name: Household glue
ratio: Gelatin 20g, Glycerin 10g, Acetic Acid 2ml
steps: Add the gelatin to hot water, heat until the gelatin is completely dissolved, add glycerin and acetic acid, and stir to use.
safetyNote: Acetic acid is irritating, please keep it ventilated during operation and avoid contact with eyes.

name: Wood glue
ratio: Gelatin 20g, Protein Powder 10g, Hot Water 50ml
steps: Add the gelatin powder to hot water, heat until the gelatin is dissolved, add the protein powder, and stir to use.
safetyNote: Please be careful of scalding when heating, it is recommended to operate under adult supervision.

name: Flour paste
ratio: 50 g flour, 200 ml water
steps: The flour is mixed with water into a uniform paste, heated over low heat and stirred continuously until it is transparent and viscous. After cooling off the fire, it is placed in a sealed container, which can be pasted with paper and lightweight materials.
safetyNote: When heating, pay attention to prevent paste and burns.

name: Rubber glue
ratio: 10g raw rubber, 50ml gasoline, 5g rosin
steps: Cut the raw rubber into granules, soak in gasoline until expanded and softened, add rosin and water to heat and stir until completely dissolved, after cooling, it can be used to repair rubber products.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated place away from open flames and children.

name: White latex
ratio: Vinyl acetate emulsion 200ml, talcum powder 10g, carboxymethylcellulose 2g
steps: Dissolve carboxymethylcellulose in a small amount of warm water to make a gel, add talcum powder and stir well, then mix with polyvinyl acetate emulsion, adjust to the appropriate consistency to use.

name: Bone Gel
ratio: 30g bone gelatin, 150ml water
steps: Bone colloids are soaked in cold water for 2 hours until expanded, heated in water and stirred until completely dissolved into a transparent form. It is used for wood splicing while it is hot, and after cooling it forms a solid bond.
safetyNote: Burn carefully when heated, glue should be dissolved and refrozen after cooling.

name: Foam
ratio: Vinyl acetate emulsion 150ml, 20g foam debris
steps: Cut the foam into finely divided granules and add to the polyvinyl acetate emulsion and stir well until the paste is homogeneous. Apply to foam or fabric adhesive surfaces and pressurize to dry.
safetyNote: Keep ventilated during bonding and do not move the adhesive until it has dried out.

name: Strong tape adhesive base
ratio: Rosin 15g, Raw Rubber 8g, Gasoline 60ml
steps: Raw rubber is shredded and soaked in gasoline, then added with rosin and stirred until completely dissolved. Apply a hot, thin coating to the tape substrate to provide strong adhesion after cooling.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated place away from open flames and children.

name: Epoxy resin adhesive
ratio: 100g epoxy, 30g curing agent
steps: Mix the epoxy resin with the curing agent according to the weight ratio, and stir thoroughly for 2 to 3 minutes until the color is even. Apply adhesive during operable time and let stand for 24 hours to fully cure.
safetyNote: After mixing, the reaction is exothermic to avoid a large amount of blending. Wear gloves during operation and keep ventilated.

name: Stamp glue
ratio: Gum Arabic 15g, Glycerin 5ml, Water 100ml
steps: Gum Arabic is dissolved in warm water, stirred well with glycerin, filtered and filled into vials. The brush is applied to the seal of the envelope or the back of the stamp, and after drying, it is firmly pasted and easy to wet and uncover.

name: Starch Binder
ratio: 50g potato starch, 400ml water, 2g boric acid
steps: The starch is mixed with water to form a suspension, heated over low heat and stirred until transparent and viscous. Add boric acid to prevent corrosion, and after cooling, it can be used for paper and carton bonding.
safetyNote: Boric acid is not edible, please mark it and store it out of reach of children.

name: Book binding glue
ratio: Animal Gum 25g, Glycerin 8g, Water 120ml
steps: After soaking the animal gum, heat and dissolve it in water. Add glycerin and stir well. Brush on spinal cloth and paper folds while hot, press-bound, and firmly bonded when cooled.
safetyNote: Burn carefully when heated, glue should dissolve now.

name: Ceramic tile adhesive
ratio: 200g cement, 400g sand, 50ml latex
steps: Dry mix the cement with the sand, add the latex and a moderate amount of water and stir until viscous. Apply the tile to the back of the wall and flatten it with a rubber hammer.
safetyNote: Cement is alkaline, please wear gloves and mask.

name: Natural varnish
ratio: Raw lacquer 50ml, rosin 10g
steps: The rosin is heated and melted in water. When the temperature is cooled to about 60° C, add raw lacquer and stir thoroughly until it is even. Can be used for carpentry stitching and lacquer closure.
safetyNote: Raw lacquer may cause skin irritation, be sure to wear gloves and operate in a ventilated place.

name: Hot melt adhesive stick
ratio: Vinyl acetate 80g, paraffin 20g
steps: The two raw materials are heated in water until they are completely melted, stirred evenly, and then poured into a rod-shaped mold. After cooling and hardening, it can be loaded into a hot melt adhesive gun for heating.
safetyNote: Heating temperature is high, pay attention to anti-scalding, keep away from children.

name: Waterproof sealant
ratio: Bitumen 100 g, kerosene 30 ml, talc 20 g
steps: After the asphalt is heated and melted, add kerosene to dilute, mix in talcum powder and mix to a suitable consistency. It is waterproof and sealed when cooled by filling in gaps or coating seams while they are hot.
safetyNote: Asphalt and kerosene are flammable, please operate in a ventilated place, away from sources of ignition.

name: Wallpaper Adhesive
ratio: Potato starch 40g, water 500ml, alum 3g
steps: The starch is mixed with water and heated over low heat to a transparent paste. Add the alum solution and stir well, cool it and apply to the back of the wallpaper and the wall with a roller.
safetyNote: Continuous stirring during heating prevents the base from being pasted.

name: Ceramic Repair Glue
ratio: Porcelain powder 20g, epoxy resin 30g, curing agent 9g
steps: The epoxy resin is mixed with a curing agent and porcelain powder is added and stirred to achieve a toothpaste-like consistency. Fix for 24 hours after filling the porcelain gap, and polish smoothly after drying.
safetyNote: After mixing, please use it during the operating time and wear gloves.

name: Perfumes
ratio: 100ml alcohol, 5g essential oil, 50ml water
steps: Pour the alcohol and water into the container, add the essential oils, stir well and leave for a while to create the perfume.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: Solid balm
ratio: 20g beeswax, 50g coconut oil, 3ml essential oil
steps: Heat the beeswax and coconut oil in water until completely melted. After leaving the fire, wait for the temperature to drop slightly. Add the essential oil and stir well. Pour into a small box and cool to set.

name: Flower Dew
ratio: Alcohol 150ml, Peppermint Brain 3g, Parfum 2ml, Water 50ml
steps: Dissolve the mint brain in alcohol, add fragrance and water, shake well, leave to stand overnight, and put in a spray bottle.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a ventilated area away from sources of ignition. Avoid contact with eyes and wounds.

name: Wardrobe fragrance bag
ratio: 30 g dry lavender, 20 g dry balsamic leaves, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Mix the dried lavender with the dried balsamic leaves, drip into the essential oils, divide into breathable cloth bags, tighten the pockets, and hang or place in the wardrobe.

name: Indoor Aromatherapy Spray
ratio: 80 ml water, 20 ml alcohol, 5 ml essential oils
steps: Mix the alcohol with the essential oil, stir well with water, and pour into a spray bottle. Shake well before use and spray in air.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: Car fragrance tablets
ratio: Diatomaceous earth 50 g, essential oil 5 ml, water 10 ml
steps: Mix diatomaceous earth with water and essential oils and press into a mold to make a sheet or block. After drying in the shade for 1 to 2 days, it is placed in the car aroma diffuser, and the aroma is slowly released with the temperature.
safetyNote: Do not place the fragrance tablets in direct sunlight, and avoid high temperatures that cause the essential oils to volatilize too quickly.

name: Bath salts
ratio: 200g sea salt, 50g baking soda, 5ml essential oil
steps: Mix the sea salt with the baking soda, drip in the essential oil, mix well, and place in a sealed jar. Take 30 grams per bath and dissolve in hot tub water.
safetyNote: If you have skin wounds or allergies, please try a small amount first to avoid direct contact with the mucous membrane.

name: Sandalwood thread
ratio: Sandalwood Powder 80g, Nanmu Powder 20g, Water 30ml
steps: Sandalwood powder is evenly mixed with camphor wood powder, gradually added with water and kneaded into a ball, and extruded with a thread incense mold. Dry in the shade for 3 to 5 days until completely dry, then ignite and use.
safetyNote: When burning, please put it in the fire-retardant incense stick, keep the room ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Arnebia sachet
ratio: 40g dried mugwort leaves, 10g cloves, 1 cotton bag
steps: Mix the dried mugwort leaves with the cloves and place them in a cotton bag to tighten the mouth of the bag. Hang indoors or by your pillow for a refreshing herbal scent.

name: Osmanthus fragrant bag
materials: brown rice  (= without its outer covering removed)
ratio: 25 g dry osmanthus, 50 g brown rice, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Mix brown rice with dried osmanthus, drip into essential oils and tie tightly in gauze bags. Store in a wardrobe or drawer for a sustainable osmanthus scent.

name: Incense cream
ratio: Agarwood Powder 10g, Sandalwood Powder 20g, Coconut Oil 40g, Beeswax 15g
steps: Heat and melt the beeswax and coconut oil separately. Add almond powder and sandalwood powder and stir well after leaving the fire. Pour into the container to cool and set, and heat a small amount in the incense burner to release the aroma.
safetyNote: Keep ventilated when incense is heated to avoid prolonged use of confined spaces.

name: Dried Floral Bag
ratio: 20 g dry rose, 20 g dry lavender, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Mix the dried flowers evenly, drizzle the essential oils and place them in a gauze bag to tie tightly. Store by the bed or in the closet for a sustainable floral scent.

name: Artemisia repellent mosquito repell
ratio: 60g mugwort powder, 20g mint powder, 20g sticky powder
steps: The three powders are evenly mixed, kneaded into a mass with an appropriate amount of water, and squeezed into a tapered or linear fragrance. Ignites after 3 days in the shade and exudes a mosquito repellent aroma.
safetyNote: When burning, place it in a fireproof container, keep it ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Jasmine dew
ratio: Fresh Jasmine 50g, Alcohol 100ml, Glycerin 10ml
steps: Jasmine is soaked in alcohol and sealed for 7 days, then filtered and shaken with glycerin. Fill with a spray bottle and use as a facial or clothing deodorant.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable and away from sources of ignition. For sensitive skin, please take a skin test first.

name: Natural Mosquito Repellent Essential Oil
ratio: Lemongrass essential oil 5 ml, Eucalyptus essential oil 3 ml, base oil 50 ml
steps: Shake the three oils well and fill them in a ball or spray bottle. Apply to exposed areas such as wrists and ankles to help repel mosquitoes.
safetyNote: Avoid contact with eyes and mucous membranes and use with caution in pregnant women and infants.

name: Sandalwood incense powder
ratio: Sandalwood powder 50g, Agarwood powder 15g, Nanmu sticky powder 35g
steps: Mix the three powders evenly and knead with water to form a pagoda fragrance or powder mass. After drying in the shade, it can be ignited in the incense burner.
safetyNote: Please keep ventilated and use fire perfume when burning.

name: Antiperspirant fragrance spray
ratio: 80 ml alcohol, 20 ml aloe vera juice, 3 ml essential oil
steps: Mix the alcohol with aloe vera juice, add the oil, shake well, and let stand for 24 hours. Fill a spray bottle and spray it under the armpits or on areas of the body that are prone to sweating.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable. Do not use if skin has wounds.

name: Laundry detergent
ratio: 50 g soda powder, 50 ml soap, moderate amount of spices
steps: Mix the soda powder with the soap solution, add the spices, and stir well to make a laundry detergent.

name: Multi-purpose washing powder
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g borax, 50g soap powder
steps: Pour baking soda, borax and soap powder into a drying vessel and stir well. Use 20-30 grams of warm water for each wash.
safetyNote: Borax is not edible and should be stored out of reach of children.

name: Hand sanitizer
ratio: 50g coconut oil soap, 10ml glycerin, 300ml warm water
steps: Dissolve the coconut oil soap in warm water, stir until completely dissolved, add glycerin and continue stirring. Cool to room temperature and load into a pressing bottle for use.

name: Clothing softener
ratio: 100 ml white vinegar, 400 ml water, 2 ml essential oils
steps: Mix the white vinegar with water, drip into the essential oil, stir well, and place in the bottle. Add 50 ml to the last rinse and soak for 5 minutes before dehydrating and drying.

name: Pipeline unblocker
ratio: 100 g baking soda, 100 ml white vinegar, 500 ml hot water
steps: First pour the baking soda into the blocked pipe, then pour into the white vinegar, react to generate bubbles, let stand for 15 minutes, and finally pour into hot water to rinse the pipe.
safetyNote: A lot of bubbles will be generated during the reaction, so please do not look at it up close. Do not mix with commercial plumbing dredges.

name: Mold Removal Detergent
ratio: 100 ml hydrogen peroxide, 20 g baking soda, 200 ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda in warm water and stir well with hydrogen peroxide. Use a soft brush to dip the mildew spot in the brush. Let it stand for 15 minutes, then rinse with water and let it dry.
safetyNote: Hydrogen peroxide is bleachable, so try to wash colored fabrics inconspicuously first.

name: Fruit and vegetable washing liquid
ratio: 15 g baking soda, 10 g salt, 1000 ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda and salt in warm water and stir well. Soak fruits and vegetables for 10 minutes, then gently scrub and rinse with water twice before serving.

name: Upper stain remover
ratio: Toothpaste 20g, baking soda 10g, warm water 50ml
steps: Mix the toothpaste with baking soda and add warm water to make a paste. Use a soft bristle brush to dip and gently brush the upper stains, wipe off the residue with a damp cloth after wiping, and dry in the shade.
safetyNote: Leather and suede materials Please try brushing in an inconspicuous place first to avoid fading or damage.

name: Hand soap with oil
ratio: 100g sawdust, 30g washing powder, 20g soap powder
steps: After the sawdust is dried, mix well with the washing powder and soap powder, and put it in the container. When washing your hands, take a proper amount of rubbing to remove the oil and oil stains on your hands, and rinse off with clean water.
safetyNote: The sawdust should be dry and mildew-free. Please use with caution if you have skin allergies.

name: Wool sweater detergent
ratio: Baby shampoo 20ml, white vinegar 10ml, cold water 2000ml
steps: Add baby shampoo and white vinegar to cold water and stir well. Flip the sweater over and soak it for 15 minutes. Gently wash it and do not rub it. Rinse it with water and dry it in the shade.
safetyNote: Do not wash with hot water to avoid shrinking wool.

name: Cutting board disinfectant
ratio: 30g salt, 1 lemon, 500ml hot water
steps: Salt is dissolved in hot water and lemon juice is mixed into the solution. Scrub the surface of the cutting board with a solution, leave it for 10 minutes and rinse with water to remove odors and light stains.
safetyNote: Pay attention to scalding during hot water operation, and wooden chopping boards should not be soaked for a long time.

name: Baby laundry detergent
ratio: 40g olive soap, 20g baking soda, 1ml lavender essential oil
steps: Dissolve the shredded olive soap in warm water and stir well with baking soda and essential oils. Cool the bottle and take 30 ml each time to wash the baby's clothes by hand.
safetyNote: Use less essential oils and rinse them off after washing.

name: Carpet dry cleaner
ratio: Cornstarch 150g, baking soda 50g, essential oil 3ml
steps: Mix cornstarch with baking soda and mix well with essential oils. Sprinkle evenly over carpet surfaces and leave for 30 minutes before vacuuming thoroughly.
safetyNote: Use a vacuum cleaner to clean the carpet before use, and try to sprinkle the dark carpet in an inconspicuous place first.

name: Coffee maker descaling solution
ratio: Citric acid 25 g, water 500 ml
steps: Citric acid dissolves in water and is poured into the tank, running a brewing procedure to pass the solution through the line. After standing for 15 minutes, rinse repeatedly with water for 2-3 times.
safetyNote: When descaling, follow the equipment instructions to prevent the solution from contacting the electrical components.

name: Leather Goods Cleaning Milk
ratio: Saddle soap 20g, lanolin 10g, beeswax 5g
steps: Saddle soap is foamed with water and mixed with lanolin and melted beeswax. Dip a soft cloth over the leather and polish with a dry cloth.
safetyNote: Try rubbing in a leather concealed area before use to avoid discoloration.

name: Denim solid lotion
ratio: 50g salt, 30ml white vinegar, 3000ml cold water
steps: Salt and white vinegar dissolve in cold water and jeans are soaked in flip for 30 minutes. Rinse with water after gentle kneading, first wash to slow fading.
safetyNote: Do not use hot water, avoid mixing with other clothes to prevent dyeing.

name: Washing machine sink cleaner
ratio: 100 g baking soda, 50 g citric acid, 500 ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda and citric acid in warm water and pour into the washing machine bucket. Run the drum self-cleaning or idling washing program, rinse it with clean water when finished.
safetyNote: Do not put clothes in when cleaning, the reaction may produce bubbles.

name: Tableware bleaching disinfectant
ratio: Bleach 10 g, Water 1000 ml
steps: Dissolve the bleaching powder in water and soak the disinfected dishes for 10 minutes. Remove and rinse repeatedly with water until there is no odor. Dry and use.
safetyNote: Bleach is corrosive, please wear gloves, not aluminum or colored cutlery.

name: Natural Lip Balm
ratio: Beeswax 10g, Coconut Oil 20g, Vitamin E 2ml
steps: Heat and melt the beeswax and coconut oil separately. Add vitamin E and stir well after leaving the fire. Pour into an empty lipstick tube to cool and set.

name: Hand Cream
ratio: Shea butter 30g, olive oil 20g, beeswax 8g, essential oil 2ml
steps: Heat and melt the beeswax in water and stir in shea butter and olive oil. After cooling down, add essential oils, whisk until creamy, and refrigerate in a jar.
safetyNote: For sensitive skin, please take a skin test first.

name: Oatmeal Honey Mask
ratio: Oat flour 30g, honey 15g, milk 30ml
steps: Mix the oatmeal with the milk and stir in the honey. Apply to face for 15 minutes then rinse with warm water and use 1-2 times a week.
safetyNote: Do not use if you are allergic to dairy or honey.

name: Brown sugar body scrub
ratio: 50 g brown sugar, 30 ml olive oil, 10 ml lemon juice
steps: Mix the brown sugar with the olive oil and stir in the lemon juice. Take a proper amount of massage when bathing, and rinse with water to smooth and moisturize the skin.
safetyNote: Do not use if skin has wounds and avoid contact with eyes.

name: Aloe Vera Moisturizing Gel
ratio: Aloe vera gel 80g, glycerin 10ml, essential oil 1ml
steps: Mix aloe vera gel with glycerin until well blended, then add essential oils and mix well. Keep refrigerated in a sealed jar for moisturizing the face or body.

name: Kitchen residue enzyme fertilizer
ratio: Fruit Peel Vegetable Leaf 300g, Brown Sugar 100g, Water 1000ml
steps: Peel chopped with brown sugar and water in a sealed bottle, leaving a third of the space. Deflate daily and dilute 500 times after 3 months of fermentation.
safetyNote: Gas is generated during fermentation, and the cap should not be tightened too tightly to avoid direct sunlight.

name: Soy Cake Liquid Fertilizer
ratio: 100g bean pie, 1000ml water
steps: After the bean cake is crushed, it is soaked in water and sealed and fermented for 15-20 days, during which it is stirred several times. Dilute the supernatant 10 times to water the plant.
safetyNote: The fermentation liquid has an odor, please operate in a ventilated place to avoid storing it indoors.

name: Plant ash potash fertilizer
ratio: 50 g grass ash, 1000 ml water
steps: The ash is soaked in water for 24 hours and the leachate is taken after filtration. Dilution of 5 times watering potassium plants promotes flowering and fruiting.
safetyNote: Grass ash is alkaline, should not be mixed with acid fertilizer, and should not be used in excessive amounts.

name: Eggshell calcium fertilizer
ratio: 50g eggshell, 50ml white vinegar, 500ml water
steps: The eggshells are washed, dried and crushed, and white vinegar is added to react to produce bubbles. After the reaction, add water to dilute and water the flowers to replenish calcium.
safetyNote: Carbon dioxide bubbles will be generated during the reaction, please operate in a ventilated place.

name: Tao Rice Water Fertilizer
ratio: 1000 ml rice water, 50 g orange peel
steps: Fill the bottle with rice water and add the pieces of orange zest. Seal fermentation for 7-10 days, dilute 3 times and water the flowers to provide nitrogen and phosphorus nutrients.
safetyNote: The fermentation time should not be too long to avoid odors and bacteria.

name: Soy Wax Scented Candle
ratio: 200g soy wax, 5ml essential oil, 1 candle wick
steps: The soy wax is heated in water and melted. After the fire, add the essential oil and stir. Fix the candle wick to the center of the container, pour in the wax liquid to cool and solidify.
safetyNote: Burn carefully when melting wax and keep away from flammable materials when ignited.

name: Beeswax Candle
ratio: 150g beeswax, 30g coconut oil, 1 candlestick
steps: Beeswax is heated in water with coconut oil until completely melted and stirred evenly. After fixing the candlestick, pour it into the mold, and cool it to release it into a candle.
safetyNote: When burning, please place it on the fire candlestick and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Smokeless Paraffin Wax Candle
ratio: 180g paraffin wax, 20g stearic acid, 1 candle wick
steps: The paraffin mixed with stearic acid is heated and melted, and poured into a mold with the candlestick fixed. Ignite when fully cooled.
safetyNote: When paraffin is heated, the temperature is high, pay attention to anti-scalding and keep ventilated.

name: Jelly Candle
ratio: 150 g jelly wax, dye in moderation, 3 ml essential oil
steps: The jelly wax is heated to 80 °C and stirred with dye and essential oils. Pour into a glass container, insert decorations and cool to set.
safetyNote: No ordinary candle wicks can be inserted into the jelly wax to ignite, only for viewing or use with LED lights.

name: Emergency oil lamp
ratio: 50ml cooking oil, 1 cotton thread, 1 metal cap
steps: The metal cap is perforated with cotton thread as the wick, and poured into the lower end of the cooking oil immersion wick. Ignite the wick to use as emergency lighting.
safetyNote: For emergency use only, keep a watchful eye when burning and keep away from flammable materials.

name: Olive oil handmade soap
ratio: Olive oil 500g, sodium hydroxide 64g, water 160ml
steps: Dissolve sodium hydroxide in water to cool, slowly pour into olive oil and stir until thick. Insulate the mold for 24 hours, and let the soap dry for 4 weeks after demolding.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be operated in a ventilated area with gloves and goggles.

name: Honey Milk Soap
ratio: 200g coconut oil, 300g olive oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 20g honey, 50ml milk
steps: After the lye cools, pour into the oil and stir, add honey and milk to continue stirring until thick. Insulate the mold, and dry the soap for 4 weeks after demolding.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled with protective equipment.

name: Coffee scrub
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 30g coffee grounds, 140ml water
steps: Stir the lye and grease until thick, add the coffee grounds and stir well. Pour in the mold and let it dry for 4 weeks after demolding. It has both cleaning and scrubbing effects when used.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and should be operated with gloves and goggles.

name: Herbal antipruritic soap
ratio: 350 g of coconut oil, 50 g of sodium hydroxide, 100 ml of herbal juice, 50 ml of water
steps: Dissolve sodium hydroxide in herba Artemisiae Argyi juice and water to cool, pour in coconut oil and stir until thick. Insulate the mold, release the mold, and dry the soap for 4 weeks to mature.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled with protective equipment in a ventilated area.

name: Activated carbon soap
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 15g activated charcoal powder, 140ml water
steps: After the lye is cooled, pour in the grease and stir, add the activated charcoal powder and stir until it is thick. Pour in the mold and dry the soap for 4 weeks to clean greasy hands.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and activated charcoal powder avoids inhalation, please wear a mask.

name: Mint deworming spray
ratio: Mint Brain 5g, Alcohol 100ml, Water 50ml
steps: Menthol is dissolved in alcohol, shaken with water and filled into a spray bottle. Spraying through the cracks and corners of doors and windows repels mosquitoes, flies, and cockroaches.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable and away from sources of ignition. Avoid contact with eyes and children.

name: Citronella repellent solution
ratio: Lemongrass essential oil 10 ml, base oil 30 ml, alcohol 60 ml
steps: Mix citronella essential oil with base oil and shake with alcohol. Apply to exposed skin or spray on clothing to help repel mosquitoes.
safetyNote: Pregnant women, infants, and people with sensitive skin should use caution and avoid contact with mucous membranes.

name: Pepper deworming bag
ratio: 30 g chili pepper, 20 g dried chili, 10 g camphor
steps: Mix the three ingredients evenly and place them in a gauze bag and tie them tightly. It is placed in the corner of the cabinet, wardrobe to repel rice insects and moths.
safetyNote: Camphor is toxic, please keep out of reach of children and not edible.

name: Natural mosquito coils
ratio: Pyrethroids 30g, sawdust 50g, sticky powder 20g
steps: Mix the ingredients evenly, add an appropriate amount of water, knead them into a ball, and press them into a mosquito incense shape with a mold. Ignite after 5 days in the shade and keep the room ventilated.
safetyNote: When burning, people should leave the room, pay attention to fire protection, and not use in confined spaces.

name: Cockroach Bait Borate
ratio: 20g boric acid, 30g sugar powder, 20g flour
steps: Mix the boric acid, sugar flour and flour evenly and add a little water to make small balls. It can be placed in the corners where cockroaches are infested to trap and kill cockroaches.
safetyNote: Boric acid is not edible and must be kept out of reach of children and pets.

name: Wood preservative oil
ratio: 100 ml creosote, 50 ml kerosene
steps: The creosote is evenly mixed with kerosene and applied to the dry wood surface with a brush. Outdoor wooden components slow decay and insect bore.
safetyNote: creosote is toxic and flammable, please wear gloves, operate in a ventilated place, away from sources of ignition.

name: Kimchi preservative brine
ratio: 100 g salt, 1000 ml water, 5 g pepper
steps: Dissolve salt in water, add chili pepper, boil, and cool. Vegetables are washed and soaked in brine and sealed for extended storage.
safetyNote: Pickled containers must be clean and sealed, and should not be eaten if they have an odor or mildew.

name: Leather mildew repellent
ratio: 80 ml alcohol, 3 ml tea tree oil, 10 ml glycerin
steps: Mix alcohol with glycerin and shake well with tea tree oil. Gently rub the leather surface with a soft cloth to prevent mildew and bacteria.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, try rubbing in a concealed area before use to avoid fading.

name: Book insect repellent
ratio: Camphor pills 20g, vanilla leaves 10g, cotton in moderation
steps: Camphor pills are mashed and mixed with vanilla leaves, wrapped in cotton and dispensed. It is placed in the bookcase and wardrobe to prevent moths from gnawing on books and clothes.
safetyNote: Camphor pills are toxic and should be kept away from children and food and not eaten by mistake.

name: Metal antirust wax
ratio: Paraffin 30g, Vaseline 40g, Oil 30ml
steps: Heat and melt paraffin in water and add petrolatum and oil and stir well. After cooling, it is coated on the metal surface to insulate it from air and rust.
safetyNote: Waste oil may contain harmful substances, please wear gloves when operating.

name: Simple fire retardant clay
ratio: Clay 200g, Salt 50g, Water 80ml
steps: The clay is evenly mixed with salt and gradually kneaded with water to a mouldable state. It can seal the pipe holes or temporarily insulate, and the hardness increases after drying.
safetyNote: Temporary protective use only, no substitute for professional fire protection materials.

name: Homemade sparkling water
ratio: 2 g baking soda, 2 g citric acid, 10 g sugar, 300 ml water
steps: Sugar dissolves in water and is filled with baking soda and citric acid respectively. Mixing before drinking produces bubbles, making it a refreshing bubble drink.
safetyNote: Drink it now, it should not be kept for a long time, and children need adult supervision to drink it.

name: Hand painted clay
ratio: 200 g flour, 100 g salt, 20 ml cooking oil, moderate amount of food coloring
steps: Mix the flour with salt, add the oil and pigment, and gradually add water and knead until the dough is non-sticky. Sealed and shaped by hand for children.
safetyNote: Although it is a food-grade raw material, it is still not edible and should be used under adult supervision.

name: Wardrobe desiccant
ratio: 50 g quicklime, 30 g calcium chloride
steps: Mix the quicklime and calcium chloride evenly, and put it in a breathable cloth bag to tighten. Suspended in a wet closet, it absorbs excess moisture and is moisture resistant.
safetyNote: The quicklime is exposed to water to release heat, do not let children touch it, and avoid being exposed to water.

name: Winter Antifreeze Glass Water
ratio: 300 ml alcohol, 10 ml dish soap, 200 ml water
steps: Mix the alcohol, dish soap and water well and fill the spray kettle. For car windshield cleaning, it is not easy to freeze at low temperatures.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, away from sources of ignition, and is intended for use only on exterior glass.

name: Rose moisturizing pure lotion
ratio: 50g rose petals, 500ml distilled water, 10ml glycerin
steps: The rose petals are distilled over small heat with distilled water or filtered after soaking for a long time, and the filtrate is added to glycerin and shaken well. Packed in a spray bottle, it can be used as a facial moisturizer or a makeup spray.
safetyNote: Off-the-shelf products have a short shelf life and are recommended to be refrigerated and used within seven days.

name: Tea Tree Acne Spot Removal Liquid
ratio: Tea Tree Essential Oil 3ml, Aloe Vera Gel 30g, Distilled Water 10ml
steps: Mix aloe vera gel with distilled water and stir in tea tree oil. Fill with a ball bottle and apply to the acne area after cleansing to avoid large areas.
safetyNote: The concentration of essential oils is high, please do a skin test first for sensitive skin, avoiding the eye area.

name: Coconut oil makeup remover
ratio: 40 g coconut oil, 8 g beeswax, 2 ml vitamin E
steps: Heat beeswax in water and melt, add coconut oil and vitamin E and stir well. After it is slightly cooled, put it in a tank to solidify, take a small amount of massage to dissolve the makeup on the face, and then rinse it with warm water emulsion.
safetyNote: Please control the amount of oil acne skin, after removal, it needs to be cleaned twice.

name: Chamomile soothing spray
ratio: 20 g dry chamomile, 200 ml distilled water, 5 ml glycerin
steps: The chamomile is soaked in hot water for 30 minutes, then filtered, and the filtrate is cooled and mixed with glycerin. Fill with a spray bottle that can be sprayed on the face after sunlight or when sensitive.
safetyNote: Do not use if you are allergic to chrysanthemum plants.

name: Sea Salt Water Styling Spray
ratio: Sea Salt 5g, Warm Water 200ml, Essential Oil 2ml
steps: Sea salt is dissolved in warm water, completely dissolved and shaken well with essential oils. Fill the spray bottle and spray on the semi-dry hair to scratch out the natural texture.
safetyNote: Suspend use if scalp has wounds or inflammation.

name: Bamboo charcoal cleansing mask
ratio: Bamboo Charcoal Powder 10g, Kaolin 20g, Water 30ml
steps: Bamboo charcoal powder is mixed with kaolin and gradually mixed with water to form a smearable mud. Apply evenly to face and rinse with warm water once or twice a week after half-drying.
safetyNote: Avoid inhalation of bamboo charcoal powder and avoid eye and lip contours when applying.

name: Vitamin E Nail Polish
ratio: 20 ml olive oil, 5 ml vitamin E, 1 ml lemon essential oil
steps: Mix olive oil with vitamin E and shake well with lemon oil. Use a small brush or cotton swab to apply to the nails and nail edges to moisturize and prevent barbs.
safetyNote: Lemon essential oil is photosensitive and avoids strong light exposure after application.

name: Mint Cool Foot Soap
ratio: Aloe vera gel 50g, menthol 2g, glycerin 5ml
steps: Menthol is dissolved with a small amount of glycerin before being thoroughly mixed with aloe vera gel. Fit the hose and apply to the feet after bathing to cool down and relieve fatigue.
safetyNote: Do not use on damaged skin and avoid contact with eyes.

name: Coffee Eye Mask
ratio: Coffee Powder 5g, Yogurt 15g, Honey 5g
steps: Coffee powder is mixed with yogurt and honey into a paste, which is applied to the eye for 10 minutes and then washed with warm water. It can temporarily relieve edema and refresh the eye area.
safetyNote: For external use only, avoid getting into the eyes, use with caution for those who are sensitive to caffeine.

name: Banana peel potassium fertilizer
ratio: Banana peel 200 g, water 800 ml
steps: The banana peel is chopped and soaked in water, sealed and fermented for 5-7 days, during which the lid is opened daily to deflate. Take the leachate and dilute it 5 times to water the potassium plants.
safetyNote: The fermentation liquid has a sour smell, please operate in a ventilated place to avoid long-term storage in the room.

name: Tea residue nitrogen fertilizer
ratio: Tea residue 100 g, water 500 ml
steps: The tea residue is soaked in water for 24 hours, and the tea solution is taken after filtration. Dilute 3 times the watering of ornamental plants to replenish nitrogen to make the leaves dark green.
safetyNote: Unfermented tea residue should not be buried directly on the surface of the basin soil to avoid mold.

name: Watermelon Skin Liquid Fertil
ratio: 300g watermelon peel, 50g brown sugar, 800ml water
steps: The watermelon rind is chopped and filled with brown sugar and water in a bottle, leaving one-third of the space for sealed fermentation. After two weeks, dilute the supernatant 10 times to water the flowers.
safetyNote: Gas is generated during fermentation, and it is necessary to regularly open the lid to deflate and avoid direct sunlight.

name: Fish scale calcium phosphate fertilizer
ratio: Fish Scales 80g, White Vinegar 100ml, Water 400ml
steps: Wash and dry the fish scales, add white vinegar to react until the bubbles weaken, then dilute with water. After standing and filtering, take the clear liquid to water the flowers, which can supplement calcium and phosphorus.
safetyNote: Gas will be generated during the reaction, please operate in a ventilated place, and do not pour waste liquid into the sewer to discharge a large amount.

name: Tobacco shredded insect repellent
ratio: 20 g shredded tobacco, 1000 ml water
steps: The tobacco shreds are soaked in water for 24 hours and the leachate is taken after filtration. Dilution of 5x spraying or rooting can assist in the control of some underground pests.
safetyNote: The leachate is toxic and should be handled with gloves to keep children out of contact with pets.

name: Crab shell phosphate fertilizer
ratio: Crab shell 100g, water 800ml
steps: The crab shells are washed, dried, crushed, and soaked in water for fermentation for about 20 days, during which they are stirred several times. Dilute the supernatant 8 times to water the flowering plants.
safetyNote: The fermentation process has an odor and is recommended to be performed outdoors or in a ventilated place.

name: Peanut withered cake
ratio: 80g groundnuts, 1000ml water, 30g brown sugar
steps: After being crushed, the peanuts are mixed with brown sugar and water for sealed fermentation for 15 days. Take the fermentation broth and dilute it 15 times to provide nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium nutrients.
safetyNote: Fertilizer should not be watered directly to avoid burning roots and should be fully diluted.

name: Deciduous compost solution
ratio: 500g fallen leaves, 200g leftovers, 1500ml water
steps: Fill the compost bucket with fallen leaves and leftovers in layers, and add water until it is wet but not watery. Turn it over once a month, and after 3 months, dilute the decomposition solution 20 times.
safetyNote: Immature compost cannot be used directly to avoid carrying germs and insect eggs.

name: Lemongrass mosquito repellent candle
ratio: 180 g soy wax, 4 ml citronella oil, 2 ml lemon oil, 1 candlestick
steps: The soybean wax is heated in water and melted. After the fire is off, add the essential oil and stir well. After fixing the candlestick, pour it into the container, cool and solidify it for a short time outdoors.
safetyNote: Keep away from flammable materials and children during ignition and do not burn in confined spaces for prolonged periods of time.

name: Layered colored candles
ratio: 240g paraffin wax, 3 colours of candle dye, 1 candle wick
steps: The paraffin melts in three portions and different dyes are added. After the first layer is slightly solidified, pour in the second layer, repeat the operation to form a layering effect, and finally insert the candle wick.
safetyNote: When melting wax, the temperature is high, pay attention to anti-scalding and keep ventilated.

name: Palm Tea Wax
ratio: 30g soy wax, 1 candle wick, 1 metal tea wax cup
steps: The soy wax is heated to melt, the candle wick is fixed in the center of the tea cup, and the wax is poured until it is full. Once cooled, it becomes a small tea wax that can be replaced repeatedly.
safetyNote: When burning, it should be placed in a fire candlestick, and people should stay away from the fire source.

name: Dried Flower Embedded Candle
ratio: Jelly Wax 120g, Dried Flowers, Essential Oil 2ml
steps: Heat the jelly wax to 80°C to melt and add the essential oils. First, pour a small amount of wax to fix the dried flowers, then fill the container, cool it down and use it as a decorative candle.
safetyNote: Dry-flower embedded candles may produce black smoke when burned, it is recommended to focus on ornamental or with LED lights.

name: Coffee Scented Candle
ratio: 200g soy wax, 10g coffee powder, 1 candle wick
steps: After melting the soy wax and leaving the fire, add the coffee powder and stir gently. After fixing the candle wick, pour it into the mold, and cool it to release the mold to give off the aroma of coffee.
safetyNote: Coffee powder particles may affect combustion stability. Please supervise the first ignition.

name: Fragrance melt wax block
ratio: 100g soy wax, 20g beeswax, 5ml essential oil
steps: Mix soy wax and beeswax and heat to melt, add essential oil and pour into the silicone grid to cool. When using, place a piece on a melted wax lamp to heat and release the aroma, no candle wick is required.
safetyNote: Do not add water when using the melted wax lamp, and keep away from flammable materials to prevent burns.

name: birthday number candles
ratio: 60 grams of paraffin wax, appropriate amount of candle dye, 1 cotton wick
steps: After the paraffin is melted, the dye is added, poured into the digital mold, and a cotton wick is inserted. Unmold after complete cooling and can be placed on the cake for a short time to light.
safetyNote: Please remove the cake from the cake before lighting it to avoid wax dripping onto the food.

name: Goat Milk Mild Soap
ratio: 350 g olive oil, 150 g coconut oil, 71 g sodium hydroxide, 160 ml goat milk
steps: Slowly dissolve the sodium hydroxide in the chilled goat's milk to cool down, pour into the mixed fat and stir until thick. Place in the mold and keep warm for 24 hours, then dry for 4 weeks after unmoulding.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be operated in a ventilated area with gloves and goggles.

name: Sea Salt Cleansing Soap
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 25g sea salt, 140ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the fat and stir until trace. Add sea salt and stir lightly to retain the graininess. Pour into molds and allow soap to dry for 4 weeks before body cleansing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid sea salt particles entering your eyes. Please wear protective equipment when operating.

name: Tea tree oil control soap
ratio: 250g coconut oil, 250g olive oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 10ml tea tree essential oil, 160ml water
steps: Stir the lye and oil until thick, add tea tree essential oil and mix well. After being put into the mold and kept warm, it is removed from the mold and allowed to mature for 4 weeks before the soap can be used for oily skin cleansing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive, and essential oils must be added at the end to avoid prolonged inhalation in a confined space.

name: red clay mineral soap
ratio: 500 g olive oil, 64 g sodium hydroxide, 20 g red clay powder, 160 ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the olive oil and stir, add the red clay powder and stir until thick. Pour into molds and allow soap to dry for 4 weeks to deeply cleanse skin.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid inhaling mineral powder. Please wear a mask.

name: Lithospermum Restorative Soap
ratio: 300g olive oil, 200g coconut oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 50g comfrey infused oil, 160ml water
steps: Stir the lye, fat and comfrey-soaked oil until trace, place into molds and keep warm. Allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks after removal, suitable for dry or slightly damaged skin.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled with protective equipment in a ventilated area.

name: Rosemary Shampoo Soap
ratio: 300g coconut oil, 100g castor oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 8ml rosemary essential oil, 140ml water
steps: Stir the lye and fat until thick, add rosemary essential oil and stir lightly. Allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks after being put into the mold. It can be used to clean short hair. It needs to be rinsed with apple cider vinegar after washing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid shampoo soap foam entering your eyes. Please wear gloves when operating.

name: beer foaming soap
ratio: 350g olive oil, 150g coconut oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 160ml beer
steps: Open the lid of the beer one night in advance to degas it, then cool it down by dissolving the alkali in it. Pour the lye solution into the fat and stir until trace, put it into the mold and keep it warm and let the soap dry for 4 weeks.
safetyNote: The reaction between sodium hydroxide and beer will produce a lot of heat and bubbles, so it must be done slowly in a ventilated area and wearing protective equipment.

name: Turmeric Warming Soap
ratio: 400 g coconut oil, 56 g sodium hydroxide, 8 g turmeric powder, 140 ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the oil and stir, add turmeric powder and mix well. Pour into molds and allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks before use in winter baths.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and turmeric is easy to stain. Please wear gloves and protect your clothing when operating.

name: Zanthoxylum bungeanum ant repellent powder
ratio: 30g Sichuan peppercorns, 20g baking soda, 50g cornstarch
steps: Dry and crush the Sichuan peppercorns and mix evenly with baking soda and cornstarch. Sprinkle it in the cracks and corners where ants often hang out to interfere with their movement routes.
safetyNote: Avoid inhalation of powder and keep away from food and reach of children.

name: Citronella repellent solution
ratio: Lemongrass essential oil 8 ml, alcohol 80 ml, water 20 ml
steps: Dissolve the essential oil in alcohol, add water, shake well, and put into a spray bottle. Spray on exposed skin or clothing before outdoor activities to repel mosquitoes for a short time.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, keep away from fire sources. Infants and children with sensitive skin please do a skin test first.

name: Garlic insect repellent soak
ratio: 50 grams of garlic, 1000 ml of water, 10 ml of soap solution
steps: Mash garlic and soak in water for 24 hours, filter and add soap solution and shake well. Spraying on plant leaves can help control soft-bodied pests such as aphids.
safetyNote: Before spraying, test spray on a small number of leaves to avoid burning the plants if the concentration is too high.

name: Tobacco water insecticide liquid
ratio: 30 grams of tobacco, 1000 ml of water, 5 ml of soap solution
steps: The tobacco is soaked in water for 48 hours, filtered, and mixed with soap. Spray on damaged plants after dilution to control some chewing pests.
safetyNote: Tobacco leachate is toxic, please wear gloves to avoid contaminating edible parts, and keep out of reach of children.

name: Grapefruit peel insect repellent bag
ratio: 100 grams of grapefruit peel, 15 grams of cloves, 1 cotton bag
steps: Cut the grapefruit peel into strips and dry them, mix them with cloves and put them into a cotton bag and tie them tightly. Place it in a rice jar, wardrobe or kitchen corner to help repel insects and prevent moths.
safetyNote: It needs to be replaced regularly to avoid moisture and mold.

name: Styrax wardrobe insect repellent tablets
ratio: 10 grams of styrax, 40 grams of camphor wood chips, 1 non-woven bag
steps: Styrax and camphor wood chips are mixed evenly, put into non-woven bags and tied tightly. Hanging in the wardrobe, it repels insects and emits a woody fragrance.
safetyNote: Keep spices out of the reach of infants and young children to avoid accidental ingestion.

name: Catnip Cockroach Repellent Powder
ratio: 20g catnip, 10g boric acid, 30g flour
steps: Crush catnip, mix it with boric acid and flour, and sprinkle it on cabinet gaps and cockroach-infested areas. It can assist in repelling and interfering with its activities.
safetyNote: Boric acid is toxic and should be kept away from children and pets. Do not spread it near food.

name: Eucalyptus oil anti-mildew spray
ratio: 5 ml of eucalyptus oil, 50 ml of alcohol, 50 ml of water
steps: Dissolve eucalyptus oil in alcohol, add water, shake well, and put into a spray bottle. Spray on wet corners and inside wardrobes to help prevent mildew and insects.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable and eucalyptus oil has a strong smell. Keep it ventilated when using it and avoid contact with eyes.

name: Beeswax Wood Care Oil
ratio: 40 g beeswax, 100 ml linseed oil, 20 ml turpentine
steps: Heat beeswax over water and melt, add linseed oil and turpentine and mix well. After cooling, apply it to the wood surface to prevent moisture and cracking and brighten it.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable and irritating, please operate in a ventilated place away from sources of ignition.

name: Tung oil anti-mildew coating fluid
ratio: 200 ml raw tung oil, 50 ml turpentine
steps: Mix raw tung oil and turpentine evenly and apply it thinly on the surface of wood or bamboo products with a brush. After drying, a protective film is formed to prevent mildew and moisture.
safetyNote: Tung oil and turpentine are both flammable. Keep away from fire sources and maintain ventilation when operating. Used brushes should be disposed of properly.

name: Silicone grain moisture-proof bag
ratio: 100 grams of dry silica gel, 2 breathable bags
steps: Put the dry silica gel granules into breathable cloth bags and tie them tightly. Place it in a camera box, bookcase or shoe box to absorb moisture and prevent moisture. It can be regenerated in the sun after being saturated.
safetyNote: Silica gel particles are not edible, please keep away from infants and young children.

name: Incense moth-proofing block
ratio: 60 grams of sandalwood powder, 40 grams of nanmu powder, 25 ml of water
steps: Mix sandalwood powder and nanmu powder, add water and knead into a ball, then dry in the shade until completely hard. Place in a bookcase or wardrobe to protect against moths and provide fragrance.
safetyNote: For moth-proofing purposes only, not for use as food or toys for children.

name: Lime drying and moisture-proof agent
ratio: 80 grams of quicklime, 40 grams of calcium chloride, 1 breathable jar
steps: Quicklime and calcium chloride are mixed and put into a breathable tank, and the lid is sealed to leave a gap. Placed in a basement or humid storage room, it can absorb ambient moisture.
safetyNote: Quicklime releases heat when it comes in contact with water. Keep out of reach of children and avoid water getting into the container.

name: Beeswax food sealing wax
ratio: 100g beeswax, 30g coconut oil, 20g resin
steps: Melt the beeswax, coconut oil and resin over water and mix well. Brush it on the surface of cheese or semi-hard food while it's hot, and it will form a removable layer of preservation wax after it cools.
safetyNote: Only suitable for direct consumption of food surfaces, make sure all ingredients are food grade before use.

name: Pine wood insect-proof strips
ratio: 200 grams of pine wood, 10 ml of camphor oil
steps: The pine wood blocks are sawn into strips and sanded, dripped or soaked in camphor oil and dried in the shade. Place in a closet, drawer or storage box to repel insects and absorb odors.
safetyNote: Camphor oil is toxic, please keep it away from children and food, and keep the container sealed.

name: homemade paste
ratio: 50g flour, 2g alum, 300ml water
steps: Mix the flour with a small amount of cold water to make a paste. Dissolve alum in the remaining water and heat it to a slight boil. Slowly pour in the batter and stir until it becomes transparent and sticky. After cooling the paper and labels can be attached.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when heating. The paste is ready for use. Do not store it for a long time to avoid deterioration.

name: Purple cabbage acid-base indicator
ratio: 100 g purple cabbage, 300 ml distilled water
steps: Chop purple cabbage and soak or lightly boil it in distilled water, then filter to get the purple filtrate. It turns red when exposed to acid and green when exposed to alkaline, and can be used for simple chemical experiments.
safetyNote: For experimental observation only, not for drinking. It is difficult to clean clothes after dyeing.

name: natural stain remover powder
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g salt, 20g lemon peel powder
steps: Mix the baking soda, salt and lemon zest powder thoroughly and place in a dusting bottle. After getting wet, sprinkle it on the sink and stove, and scrub with a damp cloth to remove light grease.
safetyNote: It cannot be used on surfaces easily corroded by alkali such as marble and aluminum products.

name: Simple concrete repair mud
ratio: 100 grams of cement, 200 grams of river sand, 60 ml of water
steps: Dry mix the cement and river sand evenly, then gradually add water and stir until it becomes plastic. Fill small holes in the wall or cracks in the floor, smooth them out and then cure them for 24 hours.
safetyNote: Cement is alkaline, so please wear gloves and a mask when operating to avoid inhaling dust.

name: emergency insulation mud
ratio: 300 grams of clay, 50 grams of straw, 100 ml of water
steps: Mix the clay with chopped straw, add water and knead until it becomes plastic. Wrapping it on the surface of outdoor water pipes or temporary facilities can slow down freezing for a short time.
safetyNote: For temporary emergencies only, it is fragile after drying and needs to be inspected and replaced regularly.

name: natural plasticine
ratio: 200g flour, 10g cream of tartar, 20ml cooking oil, appropriate amount of food coloring
steps: Mix flour and cream of tartar, add oil and coloring, gradually heat water and knead until no longer sticky. After cooling, it can be shaped by children, sealed and refrigerated.
safetyNote: Although it is a food-grade raw material, it is still not edible and needs to be used under adult supervision.

name: Simple knife sharpening paste
ratio: 20 grams of green chromium oxide powder, 30 ml of glycerin, 10 ml of water
steps: Chromium oxide powder is mixed with glycerin and water into a paste and applied to leather or wooden slats. The blade is pushed and pulled on the paste surface at a suitable angle to finely polish the blade.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling the grinding powder. Please wear a mask when operating and be careful of scratches by the blade.

name: Pipe unblocking auxiliary powder
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g citric acid, 30g salt
steps: Mix the three powders evenly and put them into sealed jars. When unblocking, pour 50 grams into the blocked pipe, add 500 ml of water, let it sit for 30 minutes and then flush.
safetyNote: A large number of bubbles will be produced during the reaction. Do not mix it with chemicals such as bleach to avoid splashing into the eyes.

name: Osmanthus infused sesame oil
ratio: 50g fresh osmanthus, 200ml olive oil, 20ml alcohol
steps: The osmanthus and olive oil are sealed and soaked for 7 days, and shaken daily. After filtering, add a small amount of alcohol to preserve it and can be used to add fragrance to your wrists or hair.
safetyNote: Freshly made infused oils are prone to oxidation, so it is recommended to be refrigerated and used within two weeks. For sensitive skin, please do a skin test first.

name: agarwood incense stick
ratio: 40 grams of agarwood powder, 60 grams of nanmu powder, 35 ml of water
steps: Mix agarwood powder and nanmu powder, gradually add water and knead until it becomes plastic, and then extrude it with an incense mold. After drying in the shade for 5 days, you can light the incense in a ventilated place.
safetyNote: When burning, please use fire-proof incense sticks, keep the room ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Jasmine fragrance wax tablets
ratio: 30g beeswax, 3ml jasmine essential oil, 2 pieces of non-woven fabric
steps: When the beeswax melts, remove from the heat and add jasmine essential oil. Dip the non-woven fabric into the wax liquid, take it out and cool it, then make fragrant wax pieces and hang them in the wardrobe to slowly release the fragrance.
safetyNote: The wax chips cannot be ignited and are only for hanging to release fragrance and avoid high temperature environments.

name: Citrus peel air freshener
ratio: 100 g orange peel, 5 g cinnamon, 5 g cloves, 500 ml water
steps: Add orange peel and spices to water and simmer over low heat for 30 minutes. After the filtrate cools, put it into a spray bottle. Spray indoors for a refreshing citrus scent.
safetyNote: Freshly prepared liquids are prone to spoilage, so it is recommended to be refrigerated and used within three days.

name: Sleep Lavender Pillow Bag
ratio: 40g dried lavender, 20g dried chamomile, 1 cotton bag
steps: Mix lavender and chamomile evenly, put into cotton bag and tie tightly. Place it next to your pillow to release the herbal aroma that helps you sleep at night.
safetyNote: Do not use if you are allergic to pollen or Asteraceae plants.

name: electric kettle descaling tablets
ratio: 40g citric acid, 20g baking soda
steps: Mix citric acid with baking soda and press into tablets or keep in powder form. Add 15 grams at a time to the kettle, add water to boil, soak for 30 minutes, rinse to remove scale.
safetyNote: Bubbles will be generated during the reaction, so the lid must not be completely sealed and cannot be used in aluminum containers.

name: leather care cleaner
ratio: 50 ml olive oil, 20 ml white vinegar, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Mix olive oil, white vinegar and essential oil, shake well, and apply a small amount to a soft cloth. Gently wipe the leather surface to remove dirt and maintain it, then polish with a dry cloth.
safetyNote: Before use, test it in a hidden area to avoid fading or leaving oil stains.

name: keyboard cleaning gel
ratio: 100 ml glue, 5 g borax, 50 ml water
steps: Borax is dissolved in water, mixed with glue and stirred until a stretchable gel forms. Press it into the gaps between the keyboard to absorb dust and can be discarded after use.
safetyNote: Borax is toxic, so please wear gloves when handling it and do not let children eat or play with it.

name: Detergent for wool
ratio: 50 g olive oil soap, 500 ml warm water, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Dissolve the soap cubes in warm water, add essential oils and stir until completely dissolved. For hand washing wool products, press gently, rinse with water, and lay flat to dry.
safetyNote: Do not rub or machine wash to avoid hot water causing the wool to shrink.

name: Gentle laundry detergent for baby clothes
ratio: 40g coconut oil soap, 20g baking soda, 800ml water
steps: Dissolve grated soap bar and baking soda in hot water and stir until clear liquid. After cooling, it can be used for hand washing of baby clothes and needs to be fully rinsed.
safetyNote: Before first use, test wash in a hidden area of ​​clothing to ensure there is no residual irritation.

name: Sportswear anti-sweat detergent
ratio: 30g baking soda, 50ml white vinegar, 1000ml water
steps: Dissolve baking soda in water, add white vinegar and shake well. Soak your sports clothing for 30 minutes before washing regularly to reduce sweat odors and mild odors.
safetyNote: Do not use it with bleach to prevent colored clothes from fading during soaking.

name: Cinnamon Apple Scented Candle
ratio: 200g soy wax, 3ml cinnamon essential oil, 2ml apple essence, 1 candle wick
steps: Heat the soy wax over water to melt, remove from the heat, add cinnamon essential oil and apple essence and stir. After fixing the candle wick, pour it into the container and let it cool and solidify.
safetyNote: When igniting, stay away from flammable materials, keep the room ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Ice crackle decorative candle
ratio: 150g paraffin wax, 50g ice wax, 1 candle wick
steps: The outer layer of paraffin is melted and poured into the mold to form the shell, and the inner layer of ice wax is melted and poured into the candle wick. After cooling, the ice wax shrinks to form a cracked decorative effect.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when melting wax, and please keep an eye on it when lighting it for the first time.

name: Container reuse candles
ratio: 150g remaining wax block, 1 candle wick, 2ml essential oil
