steps: Collect the remaining wax blocks from the burned candles and heat them over water to melt them. Filter the impurities and add essential oils. Pour into an old glass or ceramic mug and insert a new wick to cool.
safetyNote: The recycling container needs to be heat-resistant and ensure that the container is stable and does not tip over when ignited.

name: Outdoor windproof candle
ratio: 170g paraffin, 30g stearic acid, 1 candle wick
steps: Mix paraffin and stearic acid and heat until completely melted, pour into a wide-mouthed metal or thick glass container, and insert a thicker candle wick. Stearic acid can increase the melting point and is suitable for short-term outdoor use.
safetyNote: When used outdoors, it still needs to be placed on a fireproof base and kept away from flammable materials such as dry grass.

name: Snowflake texture candle
ratio: 180g soy wax, 20g white oil, 1 candle wick
steps: Mix soy wax and white oil and heat to melt. Pour into the mold and use a fork to mark the texture when the surface is semi-solidified. A snowflake-like finish develops when completely cooled.
safetyNote: The texture is for decoration only, please use it in a fireproof candle holder when burning.

name: Double wick tall candle
ratio: 300g soy wax, 50g beeswax, 2 candle wicks
steps: Mix soy wax and beeswax, heat and melt, fix two candle wicks symmetrically in a large-diameter container, and pour the wax liquid. Can extend burning time and reduce tunneling effect.
safetyNote: The double-core burning flame is larger, so please place it on a stable candlestick and keep it away from flammable objects such as curtains.

name: Linseed oil wood sealer
ratio: 150 ml flax oil, 30 ml turpentine, 20 g beeswax
steps: Heat beeswax over water and melt, add linseed oil and turpentine and stir evenly. Apply thinly to unpainted wood surfaces to seal wood pores and protect against moisture and stains.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable, keep away from fire sources and keep ventilated when handling.

name: Parchment paper food wrap
ratio: 2 sheets of parchment paper, 30 g beeswax, 10 g coconut oil
steps: Melt the beeswax and coconut oil and spread evenly on one side of the parchment paper with a brush. After cooling, it can be used to wrap cheese, bread and other foods to keep them fresh for a short period of time.
safetyNote: Only suitable for cold or room temperature food, not for high temperature heating in the oven.

name: Bamboo anti-mold oil
ratio: 100 ml raw tung oil, 15 g beeswax, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Beeswax is heated and melted and then mixed with tung oil and essential oils. Dip a soft cloth into a thin coating of bamboo cutting boards, bamboo baskets and other surfaces. After drying, it can prevent mildew and cracking.
safetyNote: Tung oil is flammable before it is completely dry, so it needs to be dried in a ventilated place away from fire sources.

name: Acid proof paper bag for books
ratio: 4 sheets of acid-free paper, 20 grams of desiccant, 1 cotton rope
steps: Each rare book is wrapped in acid-free paper and placed in an archival box with desiccant and tied tightly. It can slow down the acidification and yellowing of paper and is suitable for long-term storage.
safetyNote: The desiccant is not edible, and the file box should be placed in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.

name: Anti-rust oil for metal tools
ratio: 80 ml mineral oil, 10 g beeswax, 20 g petroleum jelly
steps: Melt the beeswax over water, add mineral oil and petroleum jelly and mix well. Apply thinly to the metal surfaces of tools such as pliers and wrenches to isolate moisture and prevent rust.
safetyNote: The surface of the tool will be slippery after oiling, so be sure to hold it stably when using it.

name: Camera lens moisture-proof box agent
ratio: 150g dry silica gel, 30g quicklime, 1 sealed box
steps: The dry silica gel granules and quicklime are packed into breathable bags and placed together at the bottom of the sealed box. The camera and lens are tightly closed after being put in to maintain a low-humidity environment.
safetyNote: Quicklime releases heat when exposed to water. Keep out of reach of children. Check the status of the desiccant regularly.

name: Sewing machine lubricating oil
ratio: 80 ml white oil, 10 g petroleum jelly
steps: Mix petroleum jelly and white oil and heat until completely combined, then cool and put into the oil pot. Drop it on the hook and guide rail of the sewing machine to reduce friction and noise.
safetyNote: Please disconnect the power supply before refueling, and use a small amount to avoid oil contamination of the fabric.

name: Bicycle chain maintenance oil
ratio: Mineral oil 60 ml, graphite powder 5 g, kerosene 20 ml
steps: Clean the chain with kerosene and dry it. Mix graphite powder and mineral oil and apply it dropwise on each link of the chain. Let it sit for a while and wipe off excess oil to prevent rust and lubricate.
safetyNote: Kerosene is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: Drawer track grease
ratio: 40 grams of petroleum jelly, 10 grams of paraffin, 10 ml of engine oil
steps: Melt the paraffin over water, add Vaseline and engine oil and stir until a smooth paste forms. Apply to wooden or metal drawer tracks to eliminate squeaking.
safetyNote: Do not use too much to avoid dripping and contaminating the furniture surface.

name: Window frame hinge lubricant
ratio: Silicone oil 30 ml, kerosene 20 ml
steps: Mix silicone oil and kerosene evenly and put into a dropper bottle. Drop it on the window hinges and hinge shafts, and repeatedly open and close them to allow the oil to seep in, which can eliminate squeaks.
safetyNote: Kerosene is flammable. Keep away from fire sources when operating to avoid dripping to the ground and causing slipping.

name: Power tool gear grease
ratio: 50 grams of lithium grease, 3 grams of graphite powder
steps: Mix graphite powder and lithium grease thoroughly until uniform. Open the power tool gearbox cover, apply a thin layer of it on the gear meshing surface, reinstall it and run it for a test run.
safetyNote: Be sure to disconnect the power supply before maintenance and operate according to the instructions. Excessive lubrication may cause overheating.

name: natural ocher pigment
ratio: 30g ocher powder, 5g gelatin, 50ml water
steps: Dissolve gelatin in warm water, gradually add ocher powder and grind until a fine slurry forms. It can be used for mural repair or hand painting, and it will turn a stable earthy red color after drying.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling dust when grinding and wear a mask.

name: Tempera paints
ratio: 20 grams of color powder, 1 egg yolk, 10 ml of water
steps: Beat the egg yolk and water, add toner in batches and grind until there are no particles. Ready to mix and use, apply thinly to wood or paper and form a soft color layer after drying.
safetyNote: Freshly made pigments are easy to deteriorate and need to be used up on the same day. Pay attention to hygiene when using raw egg yolks.

name: Lime mural background color
ratio: 100 grams of slaked lime, 50 grams of marble powder, 80 ml of water
steps: Mix hydrated lime and marble powder and gradually add water to a scrapable consistency. Apply thinly to the wall as the base color of the mural, then polish it smooth after drying.
safetyNote: Lime is alkaline, so please wear gloves and a mask when handling it to avoid getting it into your eyes.

name: natural indigo dye
ratio: 10 grams of indigo powder, 5 grams of lye, 8 grams of insurance powder, 1000 ml of water
steps: Dissolve the lye, insurance powder and indigo powder in the water in order, and stir until the reduced state turns yellow-green. After dip-dying, the fabric oxidizes and turns blue, and needs to be dip-dyed repeatedly to deepen.
safetyNote: Alkali solution and insurance powder are corrosive and must be handled in a ventilated area wearing gloves.

name: Gypsum base material
ratio: 200 grams of plaster of paris, 30 ml of white latex, 100 ml of water
steps: Make a paste of plaster of paris and water, add white latex and stir evenly. Scrape it onto canvas or wall as a base material, then polish it after drying before coloring.
safetyNote: Avoid inhalation of gypsum powder, please operate in a ventilated place when mixing.

name: Rice paste paper glue
ratio: 50g rice, 300ml water
steps: Add water to the rice and cook until soft, stir into a fine rice paste, and filter the thick liquid. It can be used to paste paper crafts and labels, and is easier to remove after drying.
safetyNote: Freshly made glue is prone to mold, so it is recommended to use it on the same day and refrigerate it for no more than two days.

name: bone glue woodworking glue
ratio: 30g bone gelatin, 150ml water
steps: Soak the bone glue particles in water for 4 hours and then heat over water until completely melted. Use it for splicing wood while it's hot, clamp it until it cools down, and the bonding strength will be higher.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when heating. The glue is very hot and requires quick operation.

name: Epoxy resin simple glue
ratio: 20 grams of epoxy resin, 5 grams of curing agent
steps: Mix epoxy resin and curing agent according to the proportion indicated and stir thoroughly until uniform. Apply to the surface of the object to be bonded within the operable time, and fix under pressure until cured.
safetyNote: Release heat after mixing, avoid skin contact, wear gloves and maintain ventilation during operation.

name: gelatin photo glue
ratio: 15 g gelatin, 5 ml glycerin, 80 ml water
steps: Dissolve gelatin in warm water, add glycerin and mix well. Apply thinly to photo album pages or handmade cards to attach photos and make them easier to remove later.
safetyNote: Do not move the paste before the glue is dry to avoid paper wrinkles.

name: Rosin flux glue
ratio: 30 g rosin, 100 ml alcohol
steps: Dissolve the crushed rosin in the alcohol and stir until completely dissolved. Apply to metal solder joints before welding to remove the oxide layer to assist soldering and improve welding quality.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, so stay away from open flames when welding, operate in a well-ventilated area and wear protective goggles.

name: Shrimp shell amino acid fertilizer
ratio: 200g shrimp shells, 50g brown sugar, 1000ml water
steps: The shrimp shells are sun-dried and crushed, then sealed with brown sugar and water for fermentation for 30 days, during which time the lid is opened and deflated every week. Dilute the supernatant 20 times and water it to supplement amino acids.
safetyNote: The fermentation liquid has a fishy smell, please operate outdoors or in a ventilated place.

name: Vermicompost organic fertilizer
ratio: 100 g of vermicompost, 500 ml of water
steps: Add vermicompost to water and soak for 24 hours, stir and then filter to obtain the fertilizer solution. Dilute it 5 times to water all kinds of potted plants, it is gentle and will not damage the roots.
safetyNote: Vermicompost should come from clean sources and avoid manure from unknown sources that may contain pathogens.

name: superphosphate leaching fertilizer
ratio: 20 g superphosphate, 1000 ml water
steps: Dissolve superphosphate in water, stir, let stand for 24 hours and then take the supernatant. Dilute 3 times to water flowering plants to promote flower bud differentiation.
safetyNote: Fertilizer solution is acidic, avoid contact with skin and eyes, and do not mix with alkaline substances.

name: Rapeseed cake liquid fertilizer
ratio: 80g rapeseed cake, 1000ml water, 30g brown sugar
steps: The rapeseed cake is crushed, mixed with brown sugar and water, and fermented in a sealed container for 15 days. Dilute the fermentation liquid 15 times and water it to provide comprehensive nutrients of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium.
safetyNote: Concentrated fertilizer must be fully diluted, otherwise it will easily burn the roots and deflate regularly during fermentation.

name: Kelp iodine fertilizer
ratio: 50 g dried kelp, 800 ml water
steps: Chop dried kelp and soak in water for 48 hours, heat to a gentle boil for 10 minutes and then filter. The filtrate is diluted 10 times and irrigated to supplement the trace element iodine.
safetyNote: Do not use too much, just use it once or twice a month.

name: Calendula Soothing Cream
ratio: Calendula infused oil 40g, beeswax 8g, shea butter 20g
steps: Heat beeswax over water and melt, add shea butter and calendula infused oil and stir. Cool and then whip until creamy. Can be used on dry or sensitive skin.
safetyNote: Please do not use if you are allergic to Asteraceae plants. Do a skin test before first use.

name: rose petal bath salts
ratio: 200 g sea salt, 20 g rose petals, 3 ml essential oil
steps: Mix sea salt and dried rose petals, drop in essential oil, mix well, and put into an airtight jar. Dissolve 30 grams in the bathtub during bathing to relax your body and mind.
safetyNote: Rinse thoroughly after bathing to avoid petals clogging the drain.

name: Green tea astringent water
ratio: 15g green tea leaves, 100ml witch hazel, 5ml glycerin
steps: Green tea leaves are soaked in hot water for 20 minutes and then filtered. The filtrate is cooled and mixed with witch hazel and glycerin. Put it in a bottle and use it as an astringent for oily skin.
safetyNote: Freshly made products have a short shelf life and are recommended to be refrigerated and used within seven days.

name: Shea Butter Foot Cream
ratio: 40g shea butter, 10g beeswax, 1g menthol
steps: Melt beeswax and shea butter over water, remove from heat, add menthol and stir. Cool and put into jar. Apply to feet before going to bed to moisturize and prevent cracks.
safetyNote: Do not use if there is a wound on the skin, and avoid contact of menthol with eyes.

name: Oatmeal mild soap
ratio: 500g olive oil, 30g oat flour, 64g sodium hydroxide, 160ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the olive oil and stir until trace. Add the oat flour and stir lightly. After placing in the mold and keeping it warm, remove it from the mold and let it dry for 4 weeks. It is suitable for bathing with sensitive skin.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled with gloves and goggles.

name: Bamboo Charcoal Deep Cleansing Soap
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 15g bamboo charcoal powder, 56g sodium hydroxide, 140ml water
steps: Stir lye and coconut oil until thick, add bamboo charcoal powder and mix well. Pour into molds and allow soap to cleanse oily skin after 4 weeks.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid inhaling bamboo charcoal powder. Please wear a mask.

name: Shea Butter Super Fat Soap
ratio: 300 g coconut oil, 200 g shea butter, 71 g sodium hydroxide, 160 ml water
steps: Stir the lye and mixed fat until trace, place into molds and keep warm for 24 hours. Allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks after unmoulding. It will produce rich foam and moisturizing after washing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled with protective equipment in a ventilated area.

name: rose petal decorative soap
ratio: 500 g olive oil, 20 g rose petals, 64 g sodium hydroxide, 160 ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the olive oil and stir. Add the dried rose petals and stir gently to retain the flower shape. Allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks after being molded, and it can be used as bathroom decorative soap.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive, the petals need to be completely dry, and please wear gloves when operating.

name: Eucalyptus mosquito repellent spray
ratio: Eucalyptus essential oil 6 ml, alcohol 70 ml, water 30 ml
steps: Dissolve eucalyptus essential oil in alcohol, add water, shake well, and put into a spray bottle. Spray on clothes or surrounding spaces before outdoor activities to repel mosquitoes for a short period of time.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable. Keep away from fire sources. Use with caution on infants and young children under three years old.

name: Rosemary anti-moth spray
ratio: 30g dried rosemary, 100ml alcohol, 50ml water
steps: Rosemary is soaked in alcohol for 7 days and then filtered, and the filtrate is diluted with water. Spray on the inside of wardrobes and storage boxes to help prevent moths.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, so stay away from fire sources when spraying and avoid contact with open flames.

name: Chili insect repellent water
ratio: 20g dried chili, 30g garlic, 1000ml water
steps: Mash the dried chilies and garlic and soak them in water for 24 hours. Filter and take the leachate. Dilute 5 times and spray on plants to repel some gnawing pests.
safetyNote: Please wear gloves when operating, avoid contact with eyes and mouth, and do not eat crops immediately after spraying.

name: Cedar Wood Insect Block
ratio: 80g cedar sawdust, 5ml essential oil, 2 non-woven bags
steps: Drip the cedar sawdust into the essential oil and mix well, then put it into a non-woven bag and tie it tightly. Placed in wardrobes, drawers for insect repellency and woody aromas.
safetyNote: Replace regularly to avoid mold in humid environments.

name: Simple fireproof blanket mud
ratio: Clay 300 g, Sand 200 g, Water 100 ml
steps: Mix clay, sand and water to form a plastic covering around the temporary welding or cutting workspace. It can block the spark splash for a short time and remove it after operation.
safetyNote: Temporary auxiliary protection only, not a substitute for professional fire protection.

name: Natural chalk
ratio: 100 g plaster, 50 ml water, moderate amount of food coloring
steps: The cooked plaster is mixed with water and pigments to form a plastic shape, filled into a chalk mold or rolled into a strip. Dry in the shade for 24 hours to completely stiff and can be used for blackboard writing.
safetyNote: Avoid inhalation of gypsum powder and do not allow children to eat it during the drying process.

name: Emergency waterproof coating
ratio: 50g paraffin, 30ml turpentine, 1 cloth
steps: After the paraffin is heated to melt, add turpentine and apply the mixture thinly to canvas or thick cloth with a brush. After cooling, it forms a waterproof layer, which can be used for temporary covering.
safetyNote: Both turpentine and paraffin are flammable and operate away from the source of ignition and with ventilation.

name: Simple abrasive polishing paste
ratio: 20g baking soda, 10g toothpaste, 5ml water
steps: Baking soda, toothpaste and water are mixed into a paste and applied to a soft cloth. It can be used to polish metal surfaces such as silver jewelry and faucets to remove mild oxidation.
safetyNote: Try rubbing in an inconspicuous place before use to avoid damaging the surface of the coating.

name: Oven degreaser
ratio: 100g baking soda, 30ml dish soap, 40ml water
steps: Baking soda is mixed with dish soap and water to form a thick paste, which is evenly applied to the oil stain on the inner wall of the oven. Wipe off with a damp cloth after standing for 2 hours, then wipe off with clean water.
safetyNote: Be sure to turn off the power and allow the oven to cool completely before cleaning, wearing gloves.

name: Shower remover
ratio: 30 g citric acid, 300 ml water
steps: Dissolve citric acid in warm water, remove the shower head and soak it for 1 hour, or soak it in a fixed shower covered with a plastic bag solution. The scale can be removed after rinsing.
safetyNote: Avoid contact with skin wounds during soaking and rinse thoroughly after cleaning.

name: Detergent for silk
ratio: Baby Shampoo 20ml, Water 500ml, Essential Oil 1ml
steps: Dilute baby shampoo in water and shake well with essential oils. For cold water hand washing silk products, gently press and rinse in clean water, do not wring in the shade.
safetyNote: Do not machine wash or expose to the sun, and test wash in a concealed place before first use.

name: Denim solid lotion
ratio: 30 g salt, 30 ml white vinegar, 3000 ml cold water
steps: Dissolve salt and white vinegar in cold water and soak the new jeans for 30 minutes. Gently rinse and dry in the shade to lighten the discoloration of the first wash.
safetyNote: For indigo dyed jeans only, do not mix with bleach.

name: Cypress incense powder
ratio: 70 grams cypress flour, 30 grams cajun flour, 25 ml water
steps: Cypress flour is mixed with cassia flour and kneaded into a cone or strip with water. After drying in the shade for 5 days, it is ignited in an incense burner to give off a woody fragrance.
safetyNote: Please use a fireproof incense burner when burning, keep ventilated, and keep people away from the source of fire.

name: Car air outlet incense clip
ratio: 2 felt, 3 ml essential oil, 1 wooden clip
steps: Cut the felt into small pieces and add essential oils to saturate. Clamped to the on-board air outlet, the air conditioner slowly releases the aroma when the air blows past.
safetyNote: Essential oils should not be in direct contact with the skin and should be replaced regularly to avoid excessive odor.
